info.title: html2md - Convert HTML pages to markdown format
info.description: A command line tool capable of converting an HTML page to markdown, downloading and re-linking any associated images.

options.name: --image-output
options.description: If no image output location is specified then they will be written to the same folder as the markdown file.

options.name: --include-tags
options.description: If unspecified the entire body tag will be processed, otherwise only text contained in the specified tags will be processed.

options.name: --exclude-tags
options.description: Allows for specific tags to be ignored.

options.name: --image-path-prefix
options.description: The prefix to apply to all rendered image URLs - helpful when you're going to be serving images from a different location, relative or absolute.

options.name: --default-code-language
options.description: The default language to use on code blocks converted from pre tags - defaults to csharp

options.name: --code-language-class-map
options.description: Map between a pre tag's class names and languages. E.g. you might map the class name "sh_csharp" to "csharp" and "sh_powershell" to "powershell".

options.name: --front-matter-data
options.description:
Allows for configuration of information to be extracted to a Front Matter property. This can be an XPath to an element or attribute in the HTML page, a string constant or a supported macro.
Supported macros:
RelativeUriPath: The relative path of the page being converted. e.g. for https://example.com/pages/page-1 the macro would return /pages/page-1

options.name: --front-matter-data-list
options.description: Allows for configuration of list-based information to be extracted to a Front Matter property.

options.name: --logging
options.description: By default no logging takes place - you can turn on logging at different levels with this flag.
